narrative.summary:
📍 Cicchetti BK
185 Howard Ave, Brooklyn, NY 11233, USA

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're deep in Bushwick on a stretch of Howard Avenue that still feels residential, walking into what reads immediately as someone's good taste made public. The room is simple—wooden tables, minimal fuss, the kind of lighting that makes everyone look like they're having a better time than they probably are. The owners are usually around, pouring wine and making recommendations like they actually care what you eat.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This is Italian small plates in the Venetian tradition—cicchetti, which means you're eating ricotta drizzled with honey on toast, not contemplating a full tasting menu. The portions are tight, the flavors are clean, and the wine list is short enough that you won't spiral into choice paralysis. You'll spend $40-60 a person if you're actually hungry and want a few glasses.

blocks.title: Cicchetti Bk
blocks.body:
📍 Cicchetti BK
185 Howard Ave, Brooklyn, NY 11233, USA

blocks.title: Arrival
blocks.body:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

Venetian wine bar in Bed-Stuy, opened March 8. Every drink comes with 3 cicchetti (tiny bites). All 15 wines by the glass are $14 each. Parmigiana di melanzane main. Stylish, intimate. Perfect hidden gem energy — brand new, barely any reviews yet.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Incredible wine and food! Such delicious small bites and a great way to start off the evening. The ricotta and honey cicchetti was a particular highlight!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "We LOVED our first visit to Cicchetti! Its simple space and straightforward menu make for a delightfully tasty night out, and I really liked that I didn't have to choose a wine from an encyclopedia. The owners are frequently there IRL and it really feels like a neighborhood spot where you can trust their recommendation and walk away with deliciousness. I didn't know that "cicchetti" is a sort of Italian tapas dish with endless variety, so we have many reasons to go back and try the next variation."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Good food and even better cocktails at reasonable prices. The meatball and the rugiada cocktails were true highlights for me. A great place to meet and chill with friends."
